task_completion.description: Create the 'Top Compute Consumers' graph using highestAverage
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Top Compute Consumers'. The query or legend should show the 'highestAverage' function with a parameter of 2 applied to compute resources (EC2 and RDS).

task_completion.description: Create the 'Critical Web Traffic' graph using averageAbove
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Critical Web Traffic'. The query or legend should indicate the 'averageAbove' function with a parameter of 60 applied to web traffic sensors.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Sorted Disk Peaks' graph using sortByMaxima
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Sorted Disk Peaks'. The query or legend should show the 'sortByMaxima' function applied to EC2 disk write metrics.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Active EC2 Fleet' graph using exclude
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Active EC2 Fleet'. The query or legend should show the 'exclude' function with the parameter 'instance_2' applied to EC2 CPU utilization metrics.
Look for the dashboard title 'Exception Monitoring' in the Graphite interface, displaying the configured graphs.

integrity.description: Agent used the Graphite web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Graphite web UI being used to construct queries and build the dashboard, not a terminal or text editor.
The graphs should render actual data lines or points, indicating real metric data is being queried.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually write the JSON result file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent typing the final dashboard JSON directly into a text editor or using echo/cat commands to bypass the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is 'Exception Monitoring'. The dashboard must contain exactly four graphs with the following titles: 'Top Compute Consumers', 'Critical Web Traffic', 'Sorted Disk Peaks', and 'Active EC2 Fleet'. The expected result file path is '/tmp/exception_based_monitoring_result.json'.
